demographics.living_situation: lives in a private residence; a concerned family member is present and made the call

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: The voices are screaming at me to do it, and the cameras in the walls won't let me sleep!
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Family member calling: 'He's been getting worse for two days, he hasn't slept, he's hearing voices and seeing things, he thinks the neighbours have cameras watching him, and he's said he wants to end it. I'm scared and I can't keep him safe.'

symptom_narrative.onset: family says it has been building fast over about 48 hours
symptom_narrative.duration: roughly two days, much worse today
symptom_narrative.character: hearing voices that give him commands, seeing things that are not there, and a fixed belief that neighbours are watching him through cameras and electronic devices
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: family says he is beside himself — pacing constantly, terrified, and not making sense
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: not applicable — it is in his mind and behaviour
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: getting steadily worse, awake almost the whole time
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing they have tried helps; he cannot be calmed
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: being alone and the lack of sleep seem to make it worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: family reports he has barely slept for three nights, his moods swing rapidly, his speech is jumbled and hard to follow, and he does not seem to realise anything is wrong
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: family does not mention anything like this happening before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: cannot look after himself, is not sleeping, and the family member feels unable to keep him safe at home

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: no regular medications; family says he is not on anything for his mind

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: family member says he does not use street drugs as far as they know

objective_data_offered.value: he is pacing the room non-stop, very restless, will not sit down, poor eye contact, and keeps talking and reacting as if to someone who is not there
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: family member's lay description over the phone, not a clinical assessment

objective_data_offered.value: his speech is very fast and pressured and he keeps losing his thread and jumping between unrelated things
the blinds are pulled shut and several electronic devices have been pulled apart and left on the floor
there are kitchen knives left out and unsecured in the next room; the family member says they are terrified and cannot manage him alone
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: reported access to means — safety-relevant, offered by the caregiver
